principles.name: Normative Vocabulary
principles.normative_text: Normative strength is expressed only with the keywords MUST, MUST NOT, SHALL, SHOULD, MAY, interpreted per RFC 2119. Every normative statement declares its strength with one of these keywords.

principles.name: Interpretation Rule
principles.normative_text: A law is interpreted by its canonical normative text. Where wording is ambiguous, the interpretation that fails closed and is more restrictive governs.

principles.name: Single Responsibility
principles.normative_text: Each law and each requirement has exactly one responsibility and exactly one canonical owner SST. Duplicate canonical ownership of the same fact is forbidden.

principles.name: Layered Precedence
principles.normative_text: The layer order Meta-Layer > Operating Laws > Machine Policies > Runtime Enforcement > Evidence/Tests is canonical. On conflict, a higher layer overrides a lower layer.

principles.name: Specificity Rule
principles.normative_text: Within one layer, the more specific requirement governs the more general one for the scope it addresses.

principles.name: Explicit Overrides Only
principles.normative_text: An override of any requirement MUST be explicit and declared at its canonical source. Silent or implicit override is forbidden.

principles.name: Conflict Detection
principles.normative_text: Two active normative requirements that remain mutually exclusive after scope, layer, specificity, and explicit-override analysis are a CONFLICT. A CONFLICT MUST stop work and be presented to the owner; it MUST NOT be resolved unilaterally.

principles.name: Law Precedence
principles.normative_text: Precedence among laws is canonical and machine-registered. Precedence is not inferred ad hoc during execution.

principles.name: Amendment Principle
principles.normative_text: The Meta-Layer changes only through a recorded amendment justified by a real new architectural requirement or a proven internal contradiction. Ordinary work does not alter it.

principles.name: Traceability Principle
principles.normative_text: Every canonical Operating Law MUST possess a complete, machine-verifiable traceability chain: Law -> Policy -> Execution Surface -> Runtime Prerequisites -> Test -> Evidence. If any required link is absent, unresolved, or fails validation, the law MUST NOT be considered enforced. Detailed requirements are defined by the canonical Operating Law Traceability Specification (OLTS); this principle carries no implementation detail.

principles.name: Verifiability Principle
principles.normative_text: A canonical statement MUST NOT claim more certainty than the available evidence proves. Every enforcement or status claim carries a Proof Level and MUST NOT exceed the minimum Proof Level of the links it depends on.

principles.name: Derivability Principle
principles.normative_text: A canonical fact SHOULD be derived from its authoritative source rather than asserted whenever derivation is possible. Enforcement status, Proof Level, laws/LAW_INDEX.md, and human documentation MUST be derived, not hand-written.
